description: Search-visible intake queue for the Thiel / Palantir / Dialog cluster and its candidate adjacency to the Clifford spine. These are QUESTIONS for public registries, not claims. Each entry names a specific bounded surface to look for and where its receipt would live. A candidate becomes a hop ONLY if a bounded, dated, co-participation surface is found in a public source; absence of a surface is a finding too (it locates the actual deniability corridor). No wrongdoing is alleged; a listing proves only a listing.

candidates.label: Peter Thiel / Palantir founder-officer surface
candidates.why_visible: Highest-confidence, fully public adjacency in the cluster; likely already promotable.
candidates.next_step: Confirm officer/founder record and dates from SEC EDGAR (Palantir S-1/10-K, CIK) and Companies House for any UK entity. Promote as a bounded founder/officer surface with primary receipts.

candidates.label: Peter Thiel / Dialog society directory-roster surface
candidates.why_visible: Reported founder/convener link to Dialog; already partly in the ledger via Wired reporting.
candidates.listing_caveat: A roster/convener listing proves a listing, not attendance, membership, endorsement, agreement, knowledge, or wrongdoing.
candidates.next_step: Confirm the bounded roster surface from the existing Wired receipts; keep as scorable context, NOT an actor-to-actor hop, unless a named co-participation event with dates is sourced.

candidates.label: Matt Clifford / Entrepreneur First founder-officer surface and portfolio adjacency
candidates.why_visible: Clifford's primary public role is EF co-founder; EF's investor base and portfolio are the most likely real bridge toward the Thiel/Palantir capital layer, if one exists.
candidates.next_step: Pull EF Companies House officer records; then check EF's disclosed investors and any Founders Fund / Thiel-linked capital participation. A hop requires a NAMED bounded co-investment/board surface with a specific EF entity, not shared sector prominence.

candidates.label: Matt Clifford / Dialog co-participation probe
candidates.why_visible: The core open question in the cluster: is there any bounded surface placing Clifford in a Dialog-adjacent venue? This is currently a hypothesis, not a link.
candidates.listing_caveat: This entry asserts NOTHING. It records that the adjacency is unverified. If no bounded co-participation surface is found in public records, that absence is the finding.
candidates.next_step: Search archived Dialog rosters and reported attendee lists for Clifford or EF. If absent, record as island_with_surfaces (surfaces exist, no valid Clifford path) rather than forcing a bridge through a broad institution.

candidates.label: Thiel-linked capital / UK AI policy machinery co-participation probe
candidates.why_visible: If a Clifford path exists, the likeliest deterministic route is capital + advisory co-participation, not social proximity. This probes whether Thiel/Palantir-linked capital and the UK AI advisory surfaces (where Clifford sits) share any bounded venue.
candidates.next_step: Cross US Senate LDA (Palantir lobbying), USASpending / UK Find-a-Tender (Palantir public contracts), and UK register-of-interests / advisory appointment records. A hop requires a named bounded surface with actor co-participation and dates. Prominence in the same sector is NOT a surface.
